tweets.author: coreyhainesco
tweets.text:
I built a skill for Claude Code that designs high-converting popups — exit intent, scroll triggers, timed delays, slide-ins, and banners.

You tell it what you want to capture and it builds the full popup spec: trigger rules, headline, offer, form fields (email only — always email only), CTA copy, dismiss text, and frequency capping so you don't annoy repeat visitors. It knows that exit-intent on desktop and time-delay on mobile are different problems with different solutions.

Done right, a sin

tweets.author: Livid
tweets.text: I use Planet, a native macOS app, as my LLM knowledge base. Just point Planet API docs to Claude Code or OpenClaw to create a skill, then you can read/write/search Planet from agents. https://t.co/jk1OM1DxCb

tweets.author: ashmaurya
tweets.text:
Most people debug with Claude Code like this:

"Fix this error."

Claude fixes the symptom. The bug comes back 2 days later in a different form.

Here's what I do instead — borrowed from Toyota's production line, adapted for AI coding:

The 5 Whys Skill.

Rule 1: Tolerate a failure only once. First time it breaks, fine. Second time, stop everything.

Rule 2: Don't ask Claude to fix it. Ask Claude to run 5 Whys.

i. Why did the page crash? → signed_id was called on an ActiveStorage blob that didn

tweets.author: braydenjlangley
tweets.text:
I used the x402 skill in Claude Code from @x402agency to pay a Whisper agent to transcribe a 52-minute Metanet Meetup video. 
It then formatted the transcript cleanly and uploaded it to UHRP for distribution, all using micropayments with no API keys or subscriptions.

https://t.co/MAJQsR5eT6

Also, the MoonShot Chrome extension MVP has been submitted and is waiting for review. :)

tweets.author: hewliyang
tweets.text:
ported over the office-agents/word toolset into a CLI

uv tool install headless-word
npx skills add hewliyang/headless-word
headless-word --help

gives any coding agent full fidelity DOCX editing including comments, track changes. 

uses LibreOffice's UNO backend https://t.co/c8jCQn942r

tweets.author: babakarto
tweets.text:
I spent weeks testing every way to bypass Seedance 2.0 face detection.
The winner?  
realistic character sheet + 8x8 white grid overlay

So I built SeedForge  
a Claude Code skill that does the entire pipeline:

  FORGE: 
Upload your photo → 
Claude analyzes every detail → 
generates the character sheet prompt to give to your Image Model (Nano banana etc)
  
SHIELD: 
Upload the sheet back → 
8x8 grid applied → 
full Seedance prompt generated
   → 
blocked? 6 alternative patterns offered automati
